summary: Part of CMP's platform team — contributing to AI feature development, design-system migrations, client bug triage, and feature requests alongside the broader platform.

bullets.d: Contributed to the platform's multi-year migration from a legacy design library to OptiAxiom, building React/TypeScript adapter components and writing migration documentation adopted across product teams.

Worked on end-to-end integration of an AI SDK — migrated the backend service, designed the internal package publishing pipeline, built the permissions layer and test suite, and wrote documentation that helped graduate the feature from beta to GA.
Built a reusable event-tracking React hook and instrumented structured AI-context events across multiple product surfaces.

Implemented backend context extraction for LLM prompts, frontend block interaction, and AI resource rendering for the platform's document AI interface — following a block-based UI pattern inspired by [Adaptive Cards](https://adaptivecards.io).
Resolved duplicate comment/task creation on rapid clicks (server + client), fixed duplicate in-app notifications, refactored Dark Mode to CSS variables, and added 8 missing i18n locale variants.
Reviewed 300+ pull requests, served as onboarding buddy for new team members, and participated in sprint planning, reviews, and technical design discussions.

summary: Worked under Dr. Denys Poshyvanyk on intelligent software tools — building NLP infrastructure for SE-specific code understanding.

bullets.d: Built a programming language-specific tokenizer to improve NLP model predictions for software engineering tasks, contributing to the lab's research on intelligent software tools.

summary: Full-stack engineering on internal tooling and a real-time stock-data integration.

bullets.d: Built custom graphs and advanced data filters to enhance reporting capabilities.
Designed and implemented a TradeStation API integration for real-time stock price data; deployed on Azure using .NET.
